acceptance_criteria_for_regrade: route_locking_review.xlsx is machine-parsable (openpyxl compatible) or accompanied by CSV/formula exports that show the same computed values.
FORMULAS sheet or plain-text formula export lists exact formulas used and the sheet/cell coordinates for route verdicts.
Memo cites specific workbook rows/cells for each route rationale.
Test-case rows are present and demonstrate the sensitivity checks described in the memo.
route_locking_review.xlsx is machine-parsable (openpyxl compatible) or accompanied by CSV/formula exports that show the same computed values.
FORMULAS sheet or plain-text formula export lists exact formulas used and the sheet/cell coordinates for route verdicts.
Memo cites specific workbook rows/cells for each route rationale.
Test-case rows are present and demonstrate the sensitivity checks described in the memo.
verification_steps: 1) Open regenerated XLSX with openpyxl in a test environment to confirm formulas and values are parseable.
2) Confirm FORMULAS sheet contains exact formula text for intermediate and final columns and references correct route row IDs.
3) Confirm CSV/formula text export loads and matches cached values present in the XLSX.
4) Update memo to include explicit workbook citations and re-run basic consistency check (routes in memo match rows in workbook).

planned_changes: Regenerate route_locking_review.xlsx using an openpyxl/xlsxwriter-compatible export path, or include an additional CSV export of computed rows (Routes.csv) so automated tooling can parse results.

Add a sheet titled FORMULAS that enumerates every formula used for intermediate and final columns, with explicit sheet/cell references (e.g., Routes!D5 = 'AND(track_clear, NOT(opposing_clear), flank_protection)') and the text of the formula as it appears in the cell.
Add explicit packet-ref columns in the main Routes sheet (e.g., packet_ref.clear, packet_ref.flank, packet_ref.track_circuit) for each route row so each computed value is traceable to a packet field.

Include the five sensitivity/test-case rows referenced in the memo as rows in the workbook with formulas and cached results; highlight or label them as TEST_CASES and add expected outcomes in an adjacent column.
Update signaling_acceptance_memo.md to reference the workbook rows/cells used to justify each route decision (e.g., 'See Routes!A5:E5 and FORMULAS!B3 for the formula used').

When manual OOXML generation is required, also export a plain-text 'FORMULAS.txt' or 'WORKBOOK_FORMULAS.csv' that lists (route_id, column_name, formula_text, cached_value) for every computed cell to ensure parseability even if openpyxl is unavailable.
Before re-submission, validate the produced artifacts locally with openpyxl (or by loading the CSV/formula outputs) to confirm automated parseability.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Automated grader could not parse the submitted XLSX (openpyxl unavailable) and therefore could not verify required visible/executable formulas and row-level packet citations (ri_1). Fixing the artifact export and making formulas/citations auditable should resolve the semantic failure.
